steps.description: Click the "Declarative Agent" option in the dropdown under the "New Project" dialog.
Click the "No Action" entry in the dropdown menu under the "Add an action to your declarative agent" header, located within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Default folder" dropdown in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to set the project root folder path (`/home/.vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects`).
Press the F1 key in the Visual Studio Code application to open the Command Palette from the main explorer view.
Type the text 'Microsoft 365 Agents: Accounts' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input box at the top of the application window.
Click the second suggestion item labeled "Microsoft 365 Agents: Accounts" in the Visual Studio Code command palette dropdown list.
Click the "Sign in to Azure" option in the account sign-in dropdown within the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Sign in" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit authentication dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Allow" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit sign-in dialog within Visual Studio Code to authorize the extension.
Click on the "Email, phone, or Skype" input field within the Microsoft sign-in dialog on the login.microsoftonline.com webpage, as indicated by the red crosshair.
Click the "Close" (X) button in the top-right corner of the Chrome window displaying the Visual Studio Code sign-in confirmation page.

Click the "New File" icon at the top of the Explorer panel in Visual Studio Code to create a new file within the current workspace folder. The interaction is indicated by the red crosshair over the document icon with a plus sign on the left sidebar.
@assertion there is "${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}}" in the ACCOUNTS section
